behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and continue through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at the parser-to-hook-handler boundary (valid parse must be consumed correctly), not just inside one parser function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: unit-mocking handler or parser could hide wiring bugs in handoff and field mapping. So Integration is the minimum real failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test wiring the real JSON parser and hook input handling path; feed valid JSON whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse succeeds, handler receives expected structured payload, and no parse-failure trace is emitted.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads emit a `json_parse_failed` trace record with diagnostic context for the malformed region.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior depends on interaction between parser error output and trace-record construction. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/OS needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking parser errors or trace sink can miss real context-shaping bugs (offset/snippet/reason mapping). Integration is the minimum to catch real diagnostic-loss failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use real parser + real trace-record builder in-process; provide malformed JSON (without synthetic trailing junk assumptions), assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes required context fields (for example position/reason/snippet) sufficient to localize the malformed segment.

behaviors.description: Local error-policy path converts parse failure into a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is primarily policy mapping logic for one local boundary (error -> handled result + telemetry intent). REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: mocks are not required; direct function/object test can expose regressions. So Unit is sufficient and minimal.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the error-policy function/object directly with a representative parse-failure error object; assert it returns handled/non-throw outcome and sets telemetry-intent flag/state exactly as expected.

behaviors.description: Under real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on subprocess stdin/exit behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked process I/O can pass while real stdin buffering, exit ordering, or flush timing fails. Therefore System is the minimum boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as a subprocess, pipe malformed JSON to stdin, wait for process completion, and assert: process does not crash unexpectedly, expected parse-failure trace signal is emitted before/at exit, and exit semantics match policy.

behaviors.description: Real hook invocation path (`stdin -> parser -> trace sink`) emits expected trace artifact for malformed input.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS + REAL-INFRA: this is an end-to-end invocation path including real stdin and trace artifact emission mechanics. MOCK-HIDE: replacing real invocation/sink can hide serialization, flush, and artifact-write failures. Minimum is System.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real hook entrypoint with malformed stdin and a real trace sink target (e.g., temp file/stdout collector used by production path); verify produced artifact contains the expected parse-failure trace record and required identifiers.

behaviors.description: Regression coverage distinguishes malformed-JSON failures from valid markdown-heavy payloads so markdown content is not falsely blamed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this distinction requires running both payload classes through real parser + failure-classification/trace path to ensure attribution is correct. Unit tests on parser alone may miss misclassification in wiring or trace labeling. No subprocess required, so Integration is minimal.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired in-process tests through parser + classification/trace pipeline: (a) valid markdown-heavy JSON must succeed with no parse-failure attribution, (b) malformed JSON must emit `json_parse_failed`; assert mutually exclusive outcomes and stable attribution labels.
